title: Regulatory delay signals lead event repricing and intraday futures drift
mechanism: Event markets underreact to high-friction regulatory delay evidence, and futures drift occurs when narrative repricing leaks into broader risk sentiment.
claim: A candidate using regulatory-delay evidence should outperform the current market or system prior on resolved event questions and should improve replay trade selection on linked futures sessions.
invalidation_conditions: No improvement versus prior across held-out event tasks
Positive result depends on a single fold or narrative cluster
Futures improvement vanishes outside trend sessions
notes: This packet is an example contract for offline replay evaluation only.
